name: Metformin decelerated multi-omics aging indicators in male monkeys
summary: A 40-month male monkey study adds non-human-primate biomarker and functional support for metformin geroscience, with important translation limits.
statement: In a 40-month adult male cynomolgus monkey study, metformin was reported to slow multi-dimensional aging indicators, including a roughly 6-year regression in brain aging, with preserved brain structure and enhanced cognitive ability.
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports a roughly 6-year regression in brain aging indicators.
caveats: This is non-human-primate evidence, not a human clinical outcome.
The abstract does not establish lifespan or mortality benefit.
The study population is male monkeys, so sex-specific and human translation remain open.
